input.prompt:
I'm starting a new console app on .NET 11 and I need to serialize this record to JSON:

```csharp
public record Person(string name, int age);
```

In the output JSON the property names should be PascalCase (so `Name` and `Age`,
not `name`/`age` or `firstName` style). I want to use whatever the framework
provides out of the box rather than writing my own naming-policy class.

Please show me the full program targeting `net11.0` and run it so I can see the
JSON output.

input.initialPrompt:
I'm starting a new console app on .NET 11 and I need to serialize this record to JSON:

```csharp
public record Person(string name, int age);
```

In the output JSON the property names should be PascalCase (so `Name` and `Age`,
not `name`/`age` or `firstName` style). I want to use whatever the framework
provides out of the box rather than writing my own naming-policy class.

Please show me the full program targeting `net11.0` and run it so I can see the
JSON output.

data.reasoningText:
The user wants a .NET 11 console app that serializes a `Person` record to JSON with PascalCase property names. They want to use the built-in `System.Text.Json` naming policy.

In .NET 9+, `System.Text.Json` added `JsonNamingPolicy.PascalCase` as a built-in naming policy. Before that, the default behavior of `System.Text.Json` was to use the property names as-is (which for C# properties would typically be PascalCase already). But since the record uses lowercase parameter names (`name`, `age`), the generated properties will actually be `name` and `age` (lowercase) because C# records generate properties matching the parameter names exactly.


I need to verify when `JsonNamingPolicy.PascalCase` was actually introduced. I know .NET 8 added `JsonNamingPolicy.SnakeCaseLower`, so I'm trying to pin down the exact version for PascalCase support. I'm not entirely sure if `PascalCase` is available as a built-in policy yet, so let me check what's actually installed in this .NET 11 environment to see what naming policies are accessible. I'll just try creating the project and testing whether `JsonNamingPolicy.PascalCase` is available in .NET 11, then adjust if needed.

data.content: .NET 11 preview SDK is available. Let me create the project and code.
